channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Student Experience Excellence Awards
item.description: The Student Experience Excellence Awards (formerly the Graduating Student Awards) recognize RPI students who have made exceptional contributions to our community through academic achievement, athletic accomplishment, leadership, service, and/or volunteerism. We invite students, staff, and faculty to nominate individuals who exemplify excellence and have made a meaningful impact on our campus.
item.author: Community

item.title: Phalanx Tapping Ceremony (RSVP)
item.description: Date: Saturday, March 28, 2026 4:30 PM - 7:00 PM - Come support the new inductees of the Phalanx Honor Society as well as White Key and Honorary Phalanx members. The Phalanx Tapping Ceremony will be held on March 28, 2026 at 4:30 PM at the Heffner Alumni House Great Hall. The event is free, but registration is required. Light appetizers and refreshments will be available.
item.author: Community

item.title: A Talk and Screening with Erica Beckman
item.description: Saturday / March 28, 2026 at 12:00 pm. As part of the lively experimental music and performance downtown scene of the 80’s, Ericka Beckman has made acclaimed works across Super-8mm, 16mm, and expanded cinema formats since the 1980’s. Engaging live-action choreographed sequences, as well as handcrafted layered animation, and face paced soundtrack, Beckman transforms the cultural norms communicated through common-place material such as fairytales and games.
item.author: Community

item.title: Sports Rules and Musicals: A Conversation with Artist Ericka Beckman
item.description: Engaging live-action choreographed sequences, as well as handcrafted layered animation, and face paced soundtrack, Beckman transforms the cultural norms communicated through common-place material such as fairytales and games. As part of the Department of Games and Experiential Media’s “Gamefest” programming and Arts’ iEar Presents, Beckman will give a talk on her work and the relationship between her art and games from 12pm-2pm on March 28 at EMPAC Theater.
item.author: Community

item.title: Graduate Students Needed to Solve National Security Problems
item.description: Earn credits this fall as you join teams to solve real problems presented by the Department of Defense. Students from all schools, including MBA students, are encouraged to register the fall Hacking 4 Defense course. Reach out to Prof. Clint Ballinger at ballic2@rpi.edu for more information. All disciplines are welcome and encouraged to register.
item.author: Community

item.title: Join us for RPI’s Watch Party for NASA’s Artemis II Launch
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 5:45 AM - 11:00 AM - We warmly invite you to join RPI’s Watch Party for NASA’s Artemis II Launch. The event is scheduled for April 1, 2026, starting at 5:45 PM ET in LOW 3051 (please note that the launch time is subject to change by NASA). We will begin with a few exciting short talks/presentations along with snacks and beverages, and there will also be plenty of fun giveaways/goodies.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “Getting More Done Without Burning Out”
item.description: Imagine sprinting through a marathon of endless demands—where every step forward risks burnout unless you rethink your approach. Traditional productivity strategies can backfire, eroding focus, energy, and decision-making. Unlock sustainable productivity in Nabeela Elsayed’s Getting More Done Without Burning Out Leadercamp, and discover strategies to stay composed, focus on what matters, and inspire your team with clarity and confidence. Two times: 9:00A-10:00A & 12:00PM-1:00PM.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff In Person Workshop - Deeper Dive into the Active Learning Approaches of Jigsaw, Think/Pair/Share, & Visual Maps
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - In this workshop, we will focus on the three active learning concepts of the Jigsaw activity, Think/Pair/Share and working with Visual Maps/Charts. We will spend some time on each of these 3 approaches, discussing the concept of each and looking at examples to help you think about how you might be able to incorporate the approach into your class. Please register by March 27th using the Event URL in the See section of the calendar event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Blackboard Ultra Course View Preview Video
item.description:
The Summer 2026 semester will be using Blackboard Ultra Course View. In preparation, here is a preview video of Blackboard Ultra Course View. You will need to log in with your RPI credentials.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
